description: Non-credential sensitive-data detection, tuned for PRECISION over recall: only flag files that genuinely ARE sensitive records — not articles, docs, or code that merely mention a topic. Three high-signal routes, any of which fires a category: (a) FILENAME — a glob naming a real document type or extension (e.g. *.dcm, *1099*, eStmt*, *.qfx). The filename route runs on EVERY file including binary and oversized ones, because a filename is metadata, not content — so it never reads the bytes. (b) PATTERN — a content regex matching an actual labelled value or data format (e.g. 'SSN: 123-45-6789', a card number, 'Rx# 12345'). (c) KEYWORDS — at least minKeywordHits DISTINCT multi-word phrases that essentially only appear in real records. Both content routes run only on text files. AVOID broad single words ('tax', 'diagnosis', 'symptom', 'patient', 'rx') and broad globs ('*tax*', '*nda*', '*phi*') — they false-fire on syntax.js, agenda.md, philosophy.md, RxJS, etc. Globs are substring-style over the basename, case-insensitive; keep tokens distinctive. patterns are JavaScript RegExp sources; a leading (?i) is honored as the case-insensitive flag. This pack is meant to be extended with more real-world naming conventions.

categories.description: Real financial records — tax forms, bank/brokerage statements, payroll, financial-export file types, and labelled bank/card numbers. Named the way these are actually downloaded and saved.

patterns.note: Labelled bank account number with a value

categories.description: Real medical / health records (PHI) — medical imaging file types (DICOM/NIfTI), record/lab/visit documents, and labelled record numbers, ICD-10 codes, or Rx numbers. Never the bare words 'patient', 'diagnosis', or 'rx'.

patterns.note: Labelled medical record number with a value
Prescription (Rx) number with a value — distinct from RX/TX comms or RxJS

categories.description: Personal-identifying info and privileged legal material — labelled IDs, contact-card files, and distinctive multi-word phrases. Never bare 'confidential' or 'litigation'.

patterns.note: Labelled date of birth with a value
